label: Architecture verdict: HYBRID approach with distributed references/ files across cli-devin/cli-opencode, cross-cutting shared assets in cli-devin/assets/, and graph-metadata enhances edges. No new sk-small-model skill warranted.

RQ1 target paths: 5 new reference docs in cli-devin/references/ (context-budget.md, truncation-strategy.md, file-summarization.md, token-usage-display.md) and cli-opencode/references/ (context-eviction.md)

RQ2 target paths: 4 new reference docs in cli-devin/references/ (verification-pipeline.md, confidence-scoring.md, hard-fail-policy.md, language-commands.md) and 1 in cli-opencode/references/ (structural-validation.md)

RQ3 target paths: 4 new reference docs in cli-devin/references/ (model-profile-schema.md, tool-scoring.md, tool-demotion.md, escalation-engine.md) and 1 in cli-opencode/references/ (model-detection.md)

RQ4 target paths: 3 new JSON schemas in cli-opencode/assets/ (tool-category-schema.json, tool-allowlist-schema.json, write-approval-gate-schema.json, permissions-matrix.schema.json) and 1 reference doc (two-stage-routing.md)
Cross-cutting shared assets: model-profiles.json and escalation-providers.json in cli-devin/assets/ for graph-metadata enhances edges

Skill-advisor integration: Add trigger phrases to cli-devin SKILL.md (small model, context budget, token optimization, SWE-1.6 budget, verification pipeline, model profile, escalation, tool scoring) and cli-opencode SKILL.md (permissions matrix, tool categories, allowlist, write approval, structured permissions, two-stage routing)

Graph-metadata enhances edges: cli-devin -> cli-opencode (weight 0.3, shared model profiles/escalation), cli-opencode -> cli-devin (weight 0.3, verification pipeline), cli-devin -> mcp-code-mode (weight 0.3, tool scoring/provider config)
